new_bullets: when asked "Determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for patient S0674240. If it was administered more than 365 days ago, order a new influenza vaccine for today.", you must actually execute the FHIR GET request to retrieve the influenza vaccine records, parse the returned response to obtain the actual date of the most recent vaccine, compare that date to the current date, and only order a new vaccine if the difference is greater than 365 days—do not simply output the GET request URL without executing it and evaluating the returned data.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a patient, first retrieve the actual heart rate observations using the correct FHIR code `http://loinc.org|8867-4` and the `date` parameter with precise `ge`/`lt` values calculated from the current time, then compute and report the numeric averages for both periods—do not stop after retrieving other observation types or report that no data exists without first performing a thorough search for heart rate observations in those exact time ranges.

when asked "Check if patient S2380121 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order.
